title: The Shafarevich Conjecture for Hypersurfaces in Abelian Varieties
speaker: Will Sawin
abstract: Faltings proved the statement, previously conjectured by Shafarevich, that there are finitely many abelian varieties of dimension nn, defined over a fixed number field, with good reduction outside a fixed finite set of primes, up to isomorphism. In joint work with Brian Lawrence, we prove an analogous finiteness statement for hypersurfaces in a fixed abelian variety with good reduction outside a finite set of primes.

proof_stages.label: Shafarevich framework and target finiteness theorem
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls Faltings' Shafarevich finiteness theorem, defines good reduction for hypersurfaces in a fixed abelian variety, and states the main finiteness theorem for smooth hypersurfaces in a fixed ample class up to translation, including the dimension-three and dimension-two qualifications.

proof_stages.label: Lawrence–Venkatesh monodromy black box
proof_stages.summary: The Lawrence–Venkatesh method is formulated as a criterion that uses finiteness of possible Galois representations together with large monodromy of a smooth proper family to prove non-Zariski-density of S-integral points.

proof_stages.label: Induction obstruction and character-twisted workaround
proof_stages.summary: A naive Noetherian induction from nondensity to finiteness fails because monodromy may shrink on closed subvarieties, while hypersurfaces in abelian varieties provide many twisted cohomology representations rho_chi so that it suffices to find one character with big monodromy.

proof_stages.label: Tanakian monodromy machinery
proof_stages.summary: Kramer–Weissauer Tanakian monodromy groups are introduced via generic vanishing of character-twisted cohomology and via convolution of perverse sheaves on the abelian variety.

proof_stages.label: Single-hypersurface Tanakian computation
proof_stages.summary: For one smooth hypersurface, Kramer's characteristic-cycle results reduce the Tanakian monodromy group to a simple group in a minuscule representation, and dimension, Hodge-theoretic, and combinatorial calculations eliminate all non-classical possibilities except for the stated forbidden numerical cases.

proof_stages.label: Transfer from Tanakian to ordinary monodromy
proof_stages.summary: A larger Tanakian group over pairs of a point and a character makes both the usual family monodromy and the Tanakian monodromy normal subgroups, so the usual monodromy is forced to contain a classical group after excluding scalar-only cases.

proof_stages.label: Contradiction proof of finiteness
proof_stages.summary: Assuming infinitely many good-reduction hypersurfaces, one takes their Zariski closure in the linear system, quotients by translation stabilizers, verifies the big-monodromy theorem's hypotheses, applies the Lawrence–Venkatesh nondensity result, and contradicts the choice of the closure.

examples.name: Previously known Shafarevich classes
examples.note: K3 surfaces, del Pezzo surfaces, flag varieties, certain complete intersections, surface fibrations, and Fano threefolds are cited to place the new finiteness theorem among earlier Shafarevich-type results.
